address.addrLine: Department of Near and Middle Eastern Collections

msContents.summary: This MS. is the 67th copy of the Íqán transcribed made by Zeynu'l Mukarrabin ("the Letter Zá")and was completed on Jemadi-ul-avval 11th, A.H. 1306 (=Jan. 13th, A.D. 1889), and is an extremely accurate and trustworthy transcript.

msItem.title: Īqān
ايقان
msItem.colophon: The colophon, in the form of a diamond superimposed on a triangle, occupies the lower part of f. 127a and records this as the 67th copy made by the scribe, completed Jumádá'l-Avval 11, AH 1306 (13 January 1889)
msItem.note: Browne quotes a passage from ff. 123a-125 of this manuscript in full, describing it as important evidence for Bahá'u'lláh's relationship with Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel at the time of writing.
msItem.quote: دهند وبيس باطلر بدیده بشناسند (f. 123a) رب در ی مد و بای ره شد چناچه و بر خاشات ای عبد اتفان اند و از هرجهت آشکار مهربان و رفیقی بغایت بردبار و رایگان با فقرا مثل فقرا بودم و با علما و عظما در كمال تسلیم ورضا مع دلک فوالله الذی لا اله الآ بارى چه اظهار نمايم كه امكانرا اگر انصاف باشد طاقت این بیان نه و اين (f. 124a) عبد در أل ورود اين ارض چون فى الجمله بر بسر بردم ... حزنها كه متوالی و متواتر و فعلاً مثل غيث هاطل وارد ميشود ... فوالذی تلق اختم القول بلا حول ولا قوة الآ بالله و انا لله وانا اليه راجعون
msItem.note: Browne states it is also quoted and translated at pp. 417-418 of vol. ii of his Traveller's Narrative.

listBibl.bibl: Browne's "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bābī Manuscripts (Continuation)", in Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland (Oct., 1892): 637-710.
Browne refers the reader to his own earlier entry (BBP. 1, pp. 435-438 of this catalogue) for further particulars of the work.F. 58

ʻAbduʼl-Bahá, Smith, W. R., Browne, E. G. (1891). A traveller’s narrative written to illustrate the episode of the Baþb; edited in the original Persian, and translated into English, with an introduction and explanatory notes, by Edward G. Browne. At the University Press.

handDesc.handNote: Written in a fine naskh hand by Zeynu'l-Muqarrabín (1818 − 1903), "the Letter Zá."

history.provenance: Given to E. G. Browne by Bahá'u'lláh's sons on 20 April 1890, as Browne was leaving Acre.
history.acquisition: Passed to Cambridge University Library in 1936 (the "Browne Bequest").

recordHist.source: "Manuscript description based on. R. A. Nicholson: A descriptive catalogue of the Oriental MSS belonging to the late E.G. Browne. Cambridge, 1932. "; provenance and item notes expanded from E. G. Browne, "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bábī Manuscripts (Continued)," Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland, Oct. 1892, pp. 664-666.

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card for admissions procedures contact Cambridge University Library Admissions). Contact near_eastern@lib.cam.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript

revisionDesc.change: Draft checked and updated with additional encoding and authority control by Yasmin Faghihi.

Claude (merge) Merged content from the corresponding Browne press-mark draft record (babi_ms/original_b_nos/BBA_2.xml) into this Fihrist record: summary, item notes, physical description, hand note, and provenance history rewritten from Browne's 1892 JRAS catalogue account (pp. 664-666) - the existing origin dating already matched the colophon date given there. Person authority key reused from elsewhere in the collection where already established (E. G. Browne, Bahá'u'lláh); left blank for Bahá'u'lláh's sons, who have no existing Fihrist/VIAF key in this collection. Not yet reviewed by a cataloguer.
Julian Cook Assigned role(s) to every person having an id (Fihrist data cleanup project).
Andrew Morrison Updated key attributes using update-keys.xsl
Andrew Morrison Added summary using add-summaries.xsl
Andrew Morrison Added or modified attributes in origDate elements using fix-dates.xsl
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/bodleian/fihrist-mss/tree/master/processing/convertFihrist2Bodley.xsl
